page.content: In the analysis of sedentary behavior and physical activity, Table 1 reveals a significant decrease in daily average minutes in sedentary behavior from Baseline to Follow-up, with ID 2 showing 378.4 minutes to 267 minutes (−29%), ID 4 at 420.1 minutes to 398.9 minutes (−5%), ID 5 from 421.0 minutes to 317.2 minutes (−25%), ID 7 decreasing from 411.8 minutes to 364.6 minutes (−11%), and a Mean of 406.8 minutes to 327.7 minutes (−19%). Maximum length of sedentary bouts for ID 2 declined from 22.9 minutes to 20.3 minutes (−11%), ID 4 went from 17.9 minutes to 42.5 minutes (137%), ID 5 reduced from 14.7 minutes to 14.2 minutes (−3%), and ID 7 decreased from 28.3 minutes to 23.5 minutes (−17%), with a Mean change from 18.5 minutes to 25.7 minutes (39%). Mean minutes of moderate or vigorous PA per day for ID 2 dropped from 39.8 minutes to 20.8 minutes (−48%), ID 4 from 33.9 minutes to 20.2 minutes (−41%), ID 5 from 27.4 minutes to 22.9 minutes (−16%), ID 7 from 33.2 minutes to 27.0 minutes (−19%), and a Mean from 33.7 minutes to 21.3 minutes (−36%). Mean steps per day for ID 2 increased from 3230 to 3848 (19%), ID 4 decreased from 3039 to 2259 (−26%), ID 5 reduced slightly from 2431 to 2397 (−1%), ID 7 increased from 3086 to 3315 (7%), with a Mean change from 2890 to 2835 (−2%).

page.content: Table 2 presents co-morbidities in the case and control groups, showing Diabetes with 4 (5) in the case group and 3 (4) in the control group, Osteoarthritis at 4 (5) vs. 6 (8), High blood pressure at 15 (19) in both groups, Heart disease at 6 (8) in both groups, Lung disease at 7 (9) in both groups, Hormone replacement therapy at 4 (5) vs. 7 (9), High cholesterol at 11 (14) in the case group compared to 8 (10) in the control group, and Thyroid disease at 6 (8) in the case group versus 3 (4) in the control group.
